source_jobs.title: Strategic Projects Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DEEPTUNE

Deeptune builds training gyms for AI agents: high-fidelity simulation environments where AI learns to do real work through reinforcement learning. We work with the leading AI labs to help them train the next generation of agentic models, and our environments have already contributed to recent breakthroughs in computer use, code generation, and multi-step task completion.

We're a ~20-person team of engineers and operators from Anthropic, Scale AI, Palantir, Hebbia, Glean, and Retool, based in-person in New York City. We recently raised a $43M Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz, with participation from 776, Abstract Ventures, and Inspired Capital.

If you want to work on the hardest problems at the intersection of reinforcement learning and real-world AI deployment, we'd love to hear from you.

Read more: Fortune exclusive on our Series A https://fortune.com/2026/03/19/andreessen-horowitz-ai-startups-deeptune-series-a/ | a16z: Why we're investing in Deeptune https://a16z.com/announcement/investing-in-deeptune/




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Strategic Projects Lead to own and scale the data operations that power frontier AI training. This is a high-ownership role for someone with strong agency, comfortable making decisions with incomplete information, and capable of building processes that hold up under pressure.

As an SPL, you will own projects end-to-end, from scoping through final delivery. You'll be responsible for managing a distributed team, forecasting timelines, meeting throughput targets, and maintaining a high bar for everything that ships. You'll face ambiguous problems and are expected to solve them in ways that scale across the org. Your work will directly shape frontier models and how they're deployed.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own delivery end-to-end: You’re accountable for data delivery end to end, including deadlines, throughput, and quality

 - Solve ambiguous problems: When you find a broken or inefficient process, you will build a scalable solution that the whole org will benefit from

 - Build and lead contributor teams: Recruit, train, and manage a distributed workforce; turn new hires into high performers

 - Partner cross-functionally: Work closely with our engineers to scope technical requirements, flag blockers, and ship improvements to our pipeline infrastructure

 - Drive continuous improvement: Identify bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and quality gaps, and then build the solutions

SPLs are obsessed with both quality and velocity. You’ll combine analytical rigor, quantitative thinking, strong judgment, and creative problem-solving to move data efficiently through complex systems – often with imperfect information.




ABOUT YOU

 - You're a self-directed operator who takes ownership without being asked.

 - You'd rather build a system than do the same task twice.

 - You're motivated by the chance to shape how frontier AI gets built.

 - You are motivated by ownership, impact, and building frontier tech.

 - Your work is a craft you want to master.

 - You thrive in ambiguity and love hard problems.

 - You appreciate diverse perspectives and uncommon ideas.

 - You are excited about building in-person, 5 days a week, from our office in Union Square NYC.




IDEALLY, YOU HAVE:

 - 2+ years of experience in high-intensity, results-driven environments (startups, consulting, banking, or similar)

 - A track record of owning outcomes and delivering under pressure

 - Strong problem-solving skills, a bias toward action, and a data-driven approach to improving systems and processes

 - Clear communication skills, especially with technical teams

 - The ability to move between hands-on execution and systems-level thinking

 - An entrepreneurial mindset to quickly build from 0 to 1

 - Genuine interest in AI / ML systems

Bonus points if you have:

 - Experience in data labeling, AI data operations, or similar human-in-the-loop workflows

 - Experience leading or managing teams (even inf

source_jobs.title: Build Your Role
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DEEPTUNE

Deeptune builds training gyms for AI agents: high-fidelity simulation environments where AI learns to do real work through reinforcement learning. We work with the leading AI labs to help them train the next generation of agentic models, and our environments have already contributed to recent breakthroughs in computer use, code generation, and multi-step task completion.

We're a ~20-person team of engineers and operators from Anthropic, Scale AI, Palantir, Hebbia, Glean, and Retool, based in-person in New York City. We recently raised a $43M Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz, with participation from 776, Abstract Ventures, and Inspired Capital.

If you want to work on the hardest problems at the intersection of reinforcement learning and real-world AI deployment, we'd love to hear from you.

Read more: Fortune exclusive on our Series A https://fortune.com/2026/03/19/andreessen-horowitz-ai-startups-deeptune-series-a/ | a16z: Why we're investing in Deeptune https://a16z.com/announcement/investing-in-deeptune/




WHY THIS POSTING EXISTS

We're growing the team significantly this year, and not every role we plan to hire for is live yet. If you're excited about what we're building and think you'd be a strong addition to the team, we want to hear from you.


Whether your background is in engineering, operations, finance, or something else entirely, submit an application and tell us what role you think you should be considered for!



We'll review every submission and reach out when there's a fit.




WHAT WE LOOK FOR ACROSS THE BOARD

You've built something meaningful, whether that's a company, a product, or a critical system at a high-performing team. You're motivated by ownership and impact, not titles. You thrive in ambiguity and move fast without waiting for permission. You want to be part of a small team doing work that matters at the frontier of AI.





HOW TO APPLY

Submit your resume and application below. Please include a note on what role or function you'd like to be considered for. We'll take it from there.

source_jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DEEPTUNE

Deeptune builds training gyms for AI agents: high-fidelity simulation environments where AI learns to do real work through reinforcement learning. We work with the leading AI labs to help them train the next generation of agentic models, and our environments have already contributed to recent breakthroughs in computer use, code generation, and multi-step task completion.

We're a ~20-person team of engineers and operators from Anthropic, Scale AI, Palantir, Hebbia, Glean, and Retool, based in-person in New York City. We recently raised a $43M Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz, with participation from 776, Abstract Ventures, and Inspired Capital.

If you want to work on the hardest problems at the intersection of reinforcement learning and real-world AI deployment, we'd love to hear from you.


Read more: Fortune exclusive on our Series A https://fortune.com/2026/03/19/andreessen-horowitz-ai-startups-deeptune-series-a/ | a16z: Why we're investing in Deeptune https://a16z.com/announcement/investing-in-deeptune/



ABOUT YOU

You’re a strong developer across backend and infrastructure (Python, Go, TypeScript, etc.) with a deep interest in agentic AI.

 - We care more about what you’ve done, not how long you’ve been doing it.

 - You’ve been a founding engineer, founded your own company, or had a major impact at a top-tier company, and you’re ready to join an early-stage team.

 - You are motivated by ownership, impact, and building frontier tech.

 - Your work is a craft you want to master.

 - You thrive in ambiguity and love hard problems.

 - You appreciate diverse perspectives and uncommon ideas.

 - You are excited about building in-person, 5 days a week, from our office in Union Square NYC.




COMP & EQUITY

 - $150K–300K base

 - Very strong equity




BENEFITS

 - 100% paid medical and dental coverage

 - Professional development budget

 - Daily team lunches

 - Commuter benefits

 - Unlimited PTO




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. You submit an application that will be reviewed by a human

 2. Intro call with Jesse (said human, and founding recruiter)

 3. Technical / Culture call with a founding engineer

 4. Join us onsite for a full day hackathon, have lunch with the team, complete our final round challenge, and meet Tim, our founder

 5. Offer decision within 24 - 48 hrs

source_jobs.title: Domain Expert - Sales
source_jobs.description:
DOMAIN EXPERT - SALES



SALES EXPERTISE WE ARE LOOKING FOR

We are especially interested in experts with hands-on CRM experience across one or more of these subdomains:



Prospecting & Account Research

 - Building and enriching pipeline: importing, deduplicating, and enriching leads and contacts, with the CRM as the system of record for who is in play

 - Account and territory research, ICP-fit assessment, and trigger-event identification

 - Buying-committee mapping across economic buyer, champion, technical evaluator, and blocker, plus org-chart navigation

 - Multi-touch outreach sequencing across email, call, and social, with personalization at scale

 - Pre-call prep and account briefs grounded in prior activity, CRM history, and external signals

Lead / Opportunity Qualification

 - Applying MEDDIC, MEDDPICC, or BANT to score fit and conversion likelihood

 - Discovery that uncovers and quantifies pain, decision criteria, decision process, and timeline

 - Distinguishing genuine pipeline from happy-ears and disqualifying early, the gate that keeps the pipeline real

 - Lead scoring, routing, and SQL / opportunity acceptance against agreed criteria

 - Qualification notes and meeting briefs that hold up to AE and manager review

Pipeline & Deal Management

 - Opportunity hygiene: keeping fields, amounts, close dates, contacts, and next steps accurate and current

 - Stage progression against exit criteria, plus aging and stalled-deal management

 - Forecasting roll-ups (commit, best-case, pipeline) with stage-progression reasoning

 - Pipeline reviews, deal inspection, and slippage and risk identification

 - The core day-to-day "live in the CRM" maintenance and data-entry workflow

Deal Orchestration

 - Logging and coordinating cross-functional stakeholders: legal, security, finance, procurement, and deal desk

 - Tracking handoffs, approvals, and dependencies against the opportunity record

 - Managing security reviews, MSA and redline routing, and procurement workflows

 - Mutual action plans and close plans spanning internal and customer stakeholders

 - Keeping the opportunity record the single source of truth for deal status

Closing & Negotiation

 - Pricing, quoting, and CPQ configuration, including discounts and non-standard terms

 - Negotiation, concession trading, and value defense

 - Redlines, contract terms, and order-form execution

 - Driving stage to closed-won and handoff to onboarding or customer success

 - Win/loss capture and CRM closeout

These sub-verticals reflect the day-to-day workflow of an account executive living in the CRM, from prospecting and qualification through pipeline and deal management, deal orchestration, and close.




TOOLS & CONTEXT

An account executive runs the whole deal inside one recurring stack, and experts should be fluent across it: the CRM (system of record), sequencing, dialer, and prospecting data, conversation capture, CPQ and quoting, forecasting and pipeline inspection, contracting, a productivity suite (mail, calendar, docs, sheets, decks), and team chat. The artifacts that move through it, such as call transcripts, discovery notes, email threads, sequences, account and buying-committee maps, mutual action plans, proposals, quotes and order forms, forecast workbooks, redlines and contracts, and the CRM opportunity record, are what experts review, create, and grade.

These systems describe the same deal from different angles, and the CRM opportunity, the email thread, the calendar, the chat approvals, and the shared docs should all agree. A core part of the AE's job, and a rich source of agent error, is reconciling them: catching a stage the email contradicts, an approval claimed in chat but missing from the record, or a next step no meeting supports.

A key part of the role is helping define what "good sales judgment" looks like in practice: how to tell a genuinely qualified deal from happy-ears, how to read a buying committee and decide who to multi-thread,

source_jobs.title: Domain Expert - Accounting/Finance
source_jobs.description:
Domain Expert - Finance

We are hiring experienced accounting professionals to help evaluate, design, and improve AI systems that support real-world accounting workflows. This is a flexible, contract-based role for accountants, auditors, controllers, tax professionals, finance systems experts, and other specialists who can bring practical judgment from hands-on accounting work.

Projects in this domain involve applied accounting tasks across a wide range of organizations, industries, and systems. Work is grounded in real accounting artifacts such as financial statements, trial balances, reconciliations, workpapers, ERP exports, audit schedules, tax filings, close checklists, and management reports.



ACCOUNTING EXPERTISE WE ARE LOOKING FOR

We are especially interested in experts with experience in one or more of the following accounting subdomains:


CORPORATE ACCOUNTING / GENERAL LEDGER ACCOUNTING

 - Month-end and year-end close processes

 - Journal entries, accruals, prepaid expenses, deferrals, and adjustments

 - Balance sheet reconciliations and variance analysis

 - Trial balance review and financial statement preparation

 - Chart of accounts design and maintenance

 - Audit support and accounting policy application


CONTROLLERSHIP / CONTROLLER FUNCTION

 - Ownership or oversight of the monthly close

 - Review of financial statements and accounting schedules

 - Management of accounting policies, procedures, and internal review processes

 - Coordination across AP, AR, payroll, tax, audit, and reporting teams

 - Evaluation of accounting judgment, materiality, and process quality


TECHNICAL ACCOUNTING

 - GAAP and IFRS research and application

 - Revenue recognition, leases, stock-based compensation, consolidations, business combinations, and complex transactions

 - Accounting memo preparation and review

 - Disclosure evaluation and policy interpretation

 - Support for audit, reporting, or transaction-driven accounting decisions


SEC / EXTERNAL REPORTING

 - Preparation or review of 10-Ks, 10-Qs, 8-Ks, earnings materials, footnotes, MD&A, and supporting schedules

 - XBRL tagging and SEC reporting workflows

 - Financial statement tie-outs and disclosure controls

 - Public-company reporting processes and audit committee support


AUDIT / ASSURANCE

 - Financial statement audits, reviews, or compilations

 - Audit planning, risk assessment, testing, sampling, and documentation

 - Internal control testing and walkthroughs

 - PBC request lists, audit schedules, confirmations, and substantive testing

 - Review note resolution and workpaper quality assessment


INTERNAL AUDIT / CONTROLS

 - SOX controls, operational audits, and compliance testing

 - Control design, control testing, and remediation tracking

 - Risk assessment and process documentation

 - Coordination with finance, IT, legal, compliance, or external auditors


TAX ACCOUNTING

 - Federal, state, local, or international tax accounting

 - Tax provision, deferred taxes, uncertain tax positions, and tax planning

 - Sales tax, payroll tax, VAT, nexus, and multi-jurisdictional filing considerations

 - Tax return preparation, review, and research

 - Entity structure and transaction tax analysis


FP&A / FINANCIAL PLANNING & ANALYSIS

 - Annual budgets, rolling forecasts, and long-range plans

 - Budget vs. actuals analysis and variance commentary

 - Revenue, margin, expense, headcount, and cash flow modeling

 - Board reporting, KPI dashboards, and scenario analysis

 - Partnership with accounting teams on actuals, accruals, and reporting outputs


CAS / CLIENT ACCOUNTING OR CLIENT ADVISORY SERVICES

 - Bookkeeping, outsourced accounting, or advisory work for multiple clients

 - Monthly close, reconciliations, AP, payroll, and financial reporting

 - Client dashboards, cash flow analysis, profitability analysis, and budgeting

 - Advisory recommendations based on client financial data

 - Experience translating messy client records into reliab

notes: Deeptune is an AI startup with a strong engineering core, recently raised $43M Series A
